Name: -1_usc_students_department_university
Representative_Docs: ['USC cutting 259 staff members to tackle rising $230 million deficit The University of Southern California began cutting 259 jobs on Friday as it tackles a rising operating deficit of more than $230 million Since July 9, USC has laid off — or is in the process of terminating — 975 people throughout the university system’s nearly 24,600 faculty and staff. That tally doesn’t include 9,185 student workers. The university is “reducing and reorganizing its workforce due to cutbacks in funding and necessary budget reductions,” wrote Melissa Gerdes-Leonard, associate vice president of client services with USC’s human resources department, in an Oct. 27 letter filed with the state’s Employment Development Department. Also see: USC won’t sign Trump compact on federal funding The latest round of layoffs are happening at USC’s University Park, a leafy 229-acre main campus for the institution’s academic schools and residential buildings near Exposition Park in downtown Los Angeles. After the cuts are completed Dec. 31, USC will have shed 616 people on its main campus, or two-thirds of the workforce laid off to date. Name: 1_injury_villa_chelsea_salah
Representative_Docs: ['Man City vs. Arsenal: Gunners suffer fresh \'injury blow\' ahead of Premier League blockbuster By Matt Law | 15 Apr 2026 21:37, Last updated: 15 Apr 2026 21:46 Arsenal attacker Noni Madueke has emerged as a major doubt for Sunday\'s blockbuster Premier League contest with Manchester City at the Etihad Stadium. Madueke made his fifth Champions League start of the campaign in Wednesday\'s quarter-final second leg against Sporting Lisbon at the Emirates Stadium. However, the 24-year-old was forced off in the 63rd minute after taking a knock to his knee. Max Dowman was introduced as the England international\'s replacement, and he has now emerged as a major doubt for this weekend\'s top-of-the-table clash with Man City. Arsenal are currently top of the Premier League table, six points ahead of second-placed Man City, who have a game in hand on the Gunners. Madueke has come up with seven goals and four assists in 35 appearances this season, but he could now be facing a spell on the sidelines entering the final straight. Bukayo Saka was also again absent for the Champions League game with Sporting. "It was something that he was carrying for a while. It was an Achilles issue," said Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta ahead of the European fixture on Wednesday night when asked about his star play', "Liverpool injury update: Alexander Isak, Alisson Becker and Jeremie Frimpong latest news and return dates Sport | Football Liverpool returned to winning ways with a 2-0 victory over Aston Villa to end their four-game losing streak in the Premier League. Pressure was piling on Arne Slot after the Reds slumped to ninth in the table, but even without a number of key players, the champions were able to sweep Villa aside to move back up to third. They welcome Real Madrid - Trent Alexander-Arnold, Xabi Alonso, et al - to Anfield in midweek, before they take on Manchester City in a mouth-watering encounter at the weekend. But will they have any of their star names back from injury? Ahead of a big few weeks, here’s all the latest Liverpool injury news and return dates... Alexander Isak has endured a tough start to life on Merseyside. Subbed at half-time in Liverpool’s 5-1 win over Frankfurt in the Champions League, Slot later revealed the £125million summer signing had suffered a minor groin injury. “Alexander had to go off at half-time because he felt his groin a little bit. That's a pity,” said Slot. “I've said many times it is such a difficult balance to find with a player that missed three months.” Isak was not in the squad in the 3-2 loss at Brentford, and he also missed out on the Caraba", "Liverpool injury update: Alexander Isak, Alisson Becker and Jeremie Frimpong latest news and return dates Liverpool returned to winning ways with a 2-0 victory over Aston Villa to end their four-game losing streak in the Premier League. Name: 2_passed away_linda_jane_grandmother
Representative_Docs: ['Janet Marie Flores Birth: 1931 Death: 2025 Janet Marie Flores passed away peacefully on November 21, 2025 at the age of 94 surrounded by the love of her family. Jan was born on October 31, 1931 in San Francisco to loving parents Alice Detour and Joseph Hettrick. Jan was a loving mother, grandmother and great grandmother who found her greatest joy in her family. She had a way of making people feel welcomed. She was kind, big hearted, extremely gracious and devoted to those she loved. Her presence will be deeply missed. Jan lived a long life traveling, playing golf, bunco with her friends and one of her greatest talents was to find a treasure at garage sales. In her final years she enjoyed playing penny slots at the casino. The last nine years Jan spent in the loving care of her daughter and son in law. A special thank you to her granddaughter Samantha for making her last days warm, comfortable and full of love. Jan survived by her daughter Tina Schilling (Michael), her grandchildren Michael (Marissa), Paul (Kacie), Samantha (Carlos), and her nine great grandchildren Jackson, Aubrey, Jordan, Mikey, Ellie, Luna, Ryder, Makayla and Stella. And her friends who will forever cherish her memory. Celebration', 'Jane Marie Solaro Birth: 1934 Death: 2026 Jane passed away peacefully on January 4, 2026 at the age of 91. Jane was born in Pacific Grove, CA on December 14, 1934 and lived in San Pedro her entire life. She is preceded in death by her loving husband John Solaro and her parents Matthew Bodul & Ann (Berkovich) Bodul. Jane enjoyed the last four years at the Canterbury in Rancho Palos Verdes with her loving friends and caretakers. She is survived by her loving children, Karen Solaro Durber (Martin), Denise Solaro and John Solaro (Lynn). She was a loving grandmother to Anthony Durber (Christina), Michelle Durber Dilly (Ian), Mark Durber, Nikki Solaro, Ali Solaro Sandoval (Dustin), Jack Solaro and Max Solaro and her great grandchildren Brooklynn and Logan Dilly She is also survived by her siblings Bob Bodul (Marylou), Sharon Bodul Werth (Fred) and Matthew Bodul (Angie) as well as several nieces and nephews. She will be deeply missed and remembered by all. Graveside burial at Green Hills Cemetery on Friday, January 23, 2026 @ 2 pm. Celebration of Life to follow. Honor with Flowers In memory of Jane Marie Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Jane Marie', 'Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser Birth: 1950 Death: 2025 Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser March 16, 1950-December 15, 2025 passed away peacefully surrounded by her family at her home in San Pancho, Mexico. Survived by her husband of 41 years, Phil Schlosser, her mother Gerry, daughter Kelly Noel, son Kevin Noel and wife Jennifer and her grandchildren Trevor and Lena. She was preceded in death by her father, Wayne Henry Jordan and brother Michael Wayne Jordan. Linda was born and raised in San Rafael, CA and graduated from Terra Linda High School. Linda loved sunshine, warm weather and travel. She was an outstanding cook and loved to entertain. Her great adventure was moving to Mexico, building a wonderful home filled with warmth, family, friends and great meals. She loved traveling throughout Mexico and Italy. Her family requests that gifts in lieu of flowers be made in Linda’s name to St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital. Forever deep in my soul, The memory of your love Fills my heart And I am never alone. Honor with Flowers In memory of Linda Sue Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Linda Sue']

Name: 3_grocery_retailers_stores_consumers
Representative_Docs: ["Major UK department store chain in working from home crackdown telling staff to be at office ‘more in person’ JOHN LEWIS has told staff it wants to see more of them back in the office to ensure it can push ahead with its recovery. The employee-owned business has reminded its workers — known as “partners” — that it expects them to be at work “more in person than not”. Please provide a valid email. Your info will be used in accordance with our Privacy Policy You'll now receive top stories, breaking news, and more, straight to your email. That means being in the office, out with suppliers or visiting customers, rather than working from home. Bosses say the move is designed to improve collaboration, speed up decisions and strengthen the firm’s culture as it continues its turnaround. John Lewis, which also owns supermarket Waitrose, insisted it was not ripping up hybrid working, stressing that it remained in place. In the note to staff, it said many businesses had stronger teamwork, a better culture and improved performance from a hybrid model with more face-to-face interactions. read more on john lewis RISKY BUSINESS UK furniture brand stocked in M&S & John Lewis axes 300 jobs after collapse SHOP TO IT John Lewis is making major change in stores and shoppers will be delighted The partnership i", 'Convenience Store Market 2031 Outlook – A USD 1001 Bn Opportunity as APAC Leads Growth with Hyper Convenience Store Expansion says a 2026 Mordor Intelligence Report Hyderabad, Jan. 09, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- According to Mordor Intelligence, the convenience store market valued at around USD 704 billion in 2025, is projected to grow from USD 747 billion in 2026 to USD 1001.6 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 6%. Growth is fueled by urban migration, rising demand for quick-shopping experiences, and the integration of digital ordering, loyalty programs, and payment systems. Retailers are expanding prepared-food offerings, adding EV charging stations, and using real-time analytics to speed up restocking, boosting basket value even as fuel sales fluctuate. Active private-equity deals and M&A continue due to steady cash flows and consistent customer traffic, while regulatory incentives for alternative-energy solutions offer new revenue opportunities. Moderate competition, with top chains holding a limited share, opens room for regional and mid-tier players to gain an edge through localized strategies, diversified products, and data-driven promotions. Regional Market Outlook Asia-Pacific Asia-Pacific is emerging as the fastest-growing region, driven by rising incomes and compact urban living. Representative_Docs: ['Source details Victoria Beckham’s ‘inappropriate’ wedding dance with son Brooklyn In this photo illustration, U.K. newspaper front pages display stories and images on the Beckham family feud on Jan. 20, 2026, in London. On Monday night, Brooklyn Beckham posted a six-page statement via Instagram detailing the tension between him and his parents, David and Victoria Beckham. (Photo Illustration by Mark Case/Getty Images/TNS) A source is detailing the “inappropriate” dance Victoria Beckham shared with son Brooklyn at his 2022 wedding to Nicola Peltz, saying it left guests “deeply uncomfortable.” The 26-year-old son of Posh Spice, 51, and David Beckham claimed in a multi-part Instagram statement Monday that his mother “hijacked my first dance with my wife,” during which he said Victoria “danced very inappropriately on me in front of everyone.” “The dance was deeply uncomfortable, and people did notice at the wedding,” an insider told Us Weekly. The source claims that the fashion designer “nuzzled on Brooklyn’s neck in front of everyone and he made a face.” Brooklyn “was in shock and upset,” they continued. “It was inappropriate and weird.” But another source speaking to the outlet felt the former Spice Girl “just seemed to be having a fun time” and people were not “g', "AP Entertainment SummaryBrief at 3:08 p.m. EST Brendan Fraser's new horizons include 'Rental Family' and rental hedgehogs BEDFORD, N.Y. (AP) — “Rental Family” is Brendan Fraser’s first leading role since winning the best actor Oscar for “The Whale.” In it, the 56-year-old plays a struggling actor living in Japan who, out of desperation and curiosity, takes a job with an agency that hires out actors to fulfill roles in real people’s lives. In an interview, Fraser discusses his life since the Oscar win, his eye-opening experiences in Japan and what returning to the “Mummy” series means to him. His time in Japan included a visit to a hedgehog cafe. “Rental Family” opens Friday in theaters. Movie Review: Joel Edgerton delivers a profound, understated performance in ‘Train Dreams’ Joel Edgerton plays a logger at the beginning of the 20th century in “Train Dreams.” Clint Bentley's film is an adaptation of Denis Johnson's novella, about the toll of progress and the people left behind. Edgerton stars alongside Felicity Jones and William H. Macy in the meditative film. Name: 5_family_friendship_husband_divorced
Representative_Docs: ['Dear Abby: I left my son a message about a family emergency. He replied with a photo from Disneyland DEAR ABBY: My sister recently became very ill and had to be hospitalized for four days. When I called and told our daughter, she was concerned and supportive. Then, I called our son, “Isaac,” and left a message to please call me because it was important. He did not return my call. A couple days later, my husband left another message for Isaac to call. He finally sent us a picture of himself and his family at Disneyland in California. He was on vacation with his wife and two kids and didn’t want to be bothered. After he got back, he waited a few days to call me. I am hurt and disappointed in Isaac. We live in Texas, and they go to Disneyland three or four times a year. I think Isaac should have taken five minutes to return my call so I could fill him in about the situation with his aunt. Because of this, I no longer feel as close to my son. In fact, I feel I’m very unimportant to him. Am I wrong to be sad and disappointed? Is it common for close relatives not to respond when they are on vacation? My sister is now recovering, but her illness has taken a huge toll on her. -- DISILLUSIONED IN TEXAS DEAR DISILLUSIONED: What a rude awakening you had. That you are hurt and disappointed is', 'Asking Eric: Can mom work through tension in son’s relationship with dad so they can celebrate special birthday? Dear Eric: My son is turning 40 on December 22. My husband and I are at a quandary as to how to celebrate him. There have been issues between my husband and him over things from his childhood. We did a special trip for his older brother when he turned 40 and would like to do something special for this son’s 40th as well. Our daughter-in-law has made special plans for him and we are not included. I understand that, but I need some ideas as to how to celebrate this extra special year without rocking the boat. I love my son with all my heart, as I do all my children, and want his 40th birthday to be memorable in a positive way. Any suggestions? – Mom Who Wants to Celebrate Dear Mom: The issues your son and your husband are having may be an obstacle, but they aren’t insurmountable. And the thing that can get them on the path to resolution is the same thing that can get you answers about how to celebrate your son: open communication. It’s not within your purview to try to fix things between them, but you have your own relationship with your son and so I’d encourage you to reach out and tell him what you told me: you want to celebrate him, you know things are difficult, and you’re lookin', 'Asking Eric: After 6 years sober, man is frustrated by his family’s hold on their hurt and distrust Dear Eric: I’ve just turned 40 this past year. The last 15 years I was in a horrible drug addiction. I lied and hurt and did terrible things to a lot of people, especially my family. About eight years ago they officially disowned me. Understandable. I’ve cleaned up and got my act together six years ago. At first, I tried to force my way back into their lives, which all refuted. I lashed out, said horrible things and stopped trying to be in their lives. My mom will stop by on my birthday for 10 minutes or so and drop a card off at Christmas. As for my two older brothers and my father, it’s radio silence. I guess what I’m asking is, what do I do to fix this and fast, as I said I’ve turned 40 this year, my parents are both 70. Time is running out, and I couldn’t imagine living my life without some kind of acceptance from my father. Or knowing he did or does love me. My heart breaks at the thought, but this is a real pickle. How can I fix a problem when the ones I need to fix it with won’t talk to me? Do I just keep ignoring their existence and put on this façade that I don’t care to my wife and 4-year-old son? What picture am I painting to my son, as he’s been guilty by association yo']

Name: 7_onstage_grammy_singer_prince
Representative_Docs: ["Lorraine Ali: Bad Bunny is American; Coldplay is not. The right is selectively freaking out over the Super Bowl Chris Martin, center, and Coldplay perform during halftime of Super Bowl 50, along with BeyoncÃ©, left, and Bruno Mars, at Levi's Stadium on Feb. 7, 2016, in Santa Clara, California. (Ezra Shaw/Getty Images/TNS) President Donald Trump told the New York Post that music artist Bad Bunny was a “terrible choice” to headline the 2026 Super Bowl halftime show and that the NFL’s selection of the Puerto Rican singer and rapper sows “hatred.” Department of Homeland Security adviser Corey Lewandowski suggested that Bad Bunny loathes the U.S. “It’s so shameful that they’ve decided to pick somebody who just seems to hate America so much to represent them at the halftime game,” he told conservative podcaster Benny Johnson. Sen. Tommy Tuberville, R-Ala., said on Monday that Bad Bunny disseminates “anti-American propaganda.” The upshot: Bad Bunny (aka Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio) is an enemy of the state. An outsider who doesn’t possess American values. A Super Bowl wrecker. Heated debate around who is worthy to perform the halftime show is an American tradition (Prince, yes. The Red Hot Chili Peppers, no). But now, unsurprisingly, politics are part of that debate, so the mere fact that Bad Bu", "THE THINK PINK GALA DEBUTS MARCH 14TH 2026 AT THE PLAZA WITH BERNADETTE PETERS AND A MICHELIN-STARRED CULINARY EXPERIENCE A landmark night of glamour, generosity, and world-class artistry in support of breast cancer patients, survivors, and families NEW YORK, Jan. 12, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The Think Pink Gala will debut on Saturday, March 14th, 2026, at the iconic Plaza Hotel, marking a premier cultural milestone in the New York philanthropic season. This inaugural evening unites world-class artistry with culinary excellence to mark United Breast Cancer Foundation's landmark 25th anniversary of life-changing support. The evening features Broadway legend and three-time Tony Award winner Bernadette Peters, two-time Tony Award winner Christine Ebersole, whose legendary career continues to define the height of American musical theater and screen, and Melissa Errico, celebrated as one of the most iconic leading ladies of musical theater. The program highlights the work of Emmy and Grammy nominated conductor, composer, and musical director Judith Clurman, who leads Judith Clurman's Essential Voices USA, an acclaimed, award-winning choral ensemble and one of New York's preeminent vocal groups. Name: 8_museum_egypt_heritage_world largest
Representative_Docs: ["Egypt unveils the world's largest museum dedicated to a single civilization CAIRO (AP) — Egypt is inaugurating on Saturday the Grand Egyptian Museum, two decades in the making, with the aim of drawing more visitors to revitalize the country's tourist industry. Located just outside Cairo on the Giza Plateau which also includes the three pyramids and the Sphinx, the museum will be the world’s largest dedicated to a single civilization. It will display over 50,000 artifacts detailing life in ancient Egypt. World leaders, including monarchs, heads of states and governments, are set to attend the grand opening ceremony, according to the Egyptian presidency which touted the museum as “an exceptional event in the history of human culture and civilization.” The museum is one of the megaprojects championed by President Abdel-Fattah el-Sissi since he took office in 2014. He has embarked on massive investments in infrastructure with the aim of reviving an economy weakened by decades of stagnation and battered by the unrest that followed the 2011 Arab Spring uprising. Preparations for the grand reveal have been shrouded in secrecy. The museum, which was opened for limited visits in recent years, has been closed for the past two weeks. Name: 9_immigration_britain_british_immigrants
Representative_Docs: ['‘UK public have tougher stance on immigration and diversity than people in US’ People in the UK are less likely than their US counterparts to feel diversity strengthens society and more likely to have tougher attitudes on immigration, according to research. The findings are said to show that a common narrative about Britain being more socially liberal than America does not ring true across all issues. The National Centre for Social Research also found Reform UK voters in Britain were more likely than supporters of President Donald Trump in the US to say immigrants living in their country without permission should not be allowed to stay. While seven in 10 supporters of Mr Trump felt this way, the figure was almost eight in 10 (79%) among Reform followers. The researchers said they wanted to “better understand how public attitudes in the UK compare with those in the US, and the extent of polarisation in the two countries”. Their survey found 42% of people in the UK think there should be legal efforts to deport immigrants living here without permission, compared with just a third (33%) in the US. While almost two-thirds (64%) of respondents in the US felt diversity strengthens society, only around half (49%) of the UK public agreed. Name: 10_freezing rain_winter weather_flooding_snow
Representative_Docs: ['Spring thaw brings flood risks to Ontario waterways: Here is what you need to know Ontario conservation authorities are keeping an eye on the risk of flooding as mild temperatures melt snow this week. With a spring thaw expected this week, Ontario conservation authorities are keeping watch for possible flood risks in rivers and streams. But no warnings have been issued just yet, said Toronto and Region Conservation Authority (TRCA) spokesperson Afiya Jilani. There are 36 conservation authorities across Ontario, and most do not have any flood alerts issued as of Tuesday, March 3. According to Environment and Climate Change Canada, temperatures across Ontario will be above freezing from Wednesday, March 4 through the weekend, which will result in snow melting around the clock. And there is also a chance of rain or freezing rain to fall as some storm activity passes across the province later this week. Meteorologists predicted this stretch of warm temperatures at the end of February, and Ontario conservation authorities have been keeping watch. “Based on forecasts for later this week, river flows are expected to increase due to above zero degree temperatures and anticipated rainfall, but it remains too early to determine if this will result in a severe river floodin', 'Spring thaw brings flood risks to Ontario waterways: Here is what you need to know Ontario conservation authorities are keeping an eye on the risk of flooding as mild temperatures melt snow this week. Name: 12_racing_newman_raceway_track
Representative_Docs: ["Denny Hamlin qualifies first at Martinsville, moves into tie for 10th in NASCAR poles MARTINSVILLE, Va. — Denny Hamlin earned his fifth pole position at Martinsville Speedway and 49th of his Cup Series career, tying him with NASCAR Hall of Famer Bobby Isaac for 10th all time. The Joe Gibbs Racing star turned a 98.241-mph lap Saturday in the No. 11 Toyota to beat William Byron, whose No. 24 Chevrolet qualified second at 97.957 mph for Sunday’s 400-lap race. With his win two weeks ago at Las Vegas Motor Speedway, Hamlin, 45, broke a tie with Kevin Harvick for 10th on the all-time win list in NASCAR's premier series. He is two poles away from tying the career total of Ryan Newman, one of the greatest qualifiers in NASCAR history. “It’s pretty awesome,” said Hamlin, who grew up a few hours east of Martinsville in the Richmond, Virginia, suburb of Chesterfield. “Really with age, the hardest part is still having the fast time. It’s one thing to be able to manage races and use your experience to your advantage. But usually the first thing to go is your all-out speed, and we’re still knocking off poles, which is really good. I was around when Newman was just unstoppable in qualifying. So damn, I didn’t know he had that many.” Hamlin has six wins at Martinsville, and his vic", "Denny Hamlin qualifies first at Martinsville, moves into tie for 10th in NASCAR poles MARTINSVILLE, Va. — Denny Hamlin earned his fifth pole position at Martinsville Speedway and 49th of his Cup Series career, tying him with NASCAR Hall of Famer Bobby Isaac for 10th all time. Name: 14_fentanyl_drugs_marijuana_substances
Representative_Docs: ['DEA warns fentanyl mixtures overwhelming overdose reversal drug (The Center Square) – The U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration warned Americans Tuesday that fentanyl is increasingly mixed with a dangerous array of synthetic substances that can limit the effectiveness of naloxone, the standard overdose reversal drug, making the illicit drug supply more unpredictable and more lethal than ever. Law enforcement and public health officials are seeing fentanyl combined with xylazine, medetomidine, nitazenes and cychlorphine – substances that either cannot be reversed by naloxone or require multiple doses to counter, the DEA said in a public safety advisory. Users typically have no way of knowing what is in the drugs they are taking. The advisory arrives as the DEA and the Trump administration have been touting significant progress against fentanyl. Enforcement pressure drove the share of fentanyl pills containing a potentially lethal dose from 76% in fiscal 2023 to 29% in fiscal 2025, a result the agency has repeatedly cited as a win. But the DEA\'s own 2025 National Drug Threat Assessment warned that the declining purity trend "does not mean that street-level fentanyl is less dangerous," pointing directly to adulteration as the compensating threat. Representative_Docs: ['VIDEO: $5.5M robotic surgery system arrives at Surrey Memorial Hospital Published 12:10 pm Tuesday, January 27, 2026 By Anna Burns The da Vinci Xi System has been unpacked and calibrated and is being held in a temporary location until its permanent location is finished. Fraser Health said that it expects it to “go-live” later in the spring. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Health care workers pose in front of the da Vinci Xi System. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Surrey Memorial Hospital is one step closer to providing robotic-assisted surgery with the arrival of the da Vinci Xi Robotic Surgery system. It is expected to be used in surgeries in the spring. “With the aid of the da Vinci Xi system, we can expand minimally invasive thoracic and foregut surgery, and we can do operations with greater precision and accuracy,” says Dr Ahmad Ashrafi, regional division head and chief of thoracic surgery for Fraser Health. “Patients may experience quicker recovery times, and we can carry out more complex procedures right here in Surrey, using minimally invasive techniques. Name: 16_creams_surgery_injections_cardiovascular
Representative_Docs: ['Could AI help treat people with prostate cancer? A groundbreaking study is set to find out A new study is set to investigate how AI could significantly improve doctors’ decisions regarding prostate cancer treatment. While diagnostic methods for the disease have become safer and more precise, medical professionals still face considerable challenges in accurately assessing its aggressiveness in individual patients. This difficulty can lead to some men undergoing invasive procedures such as surgery or radiotherapy, when a less aggressive monitoring approach might have been more appropriate. The crucial Vanguard Path study, spearheaded by researchers at the University of Oxford, is being funded with a £1.9m grant from the charity Prostate Cancer UK to address this vital issue. Experts will first test the technology – called ArteraAI Prostate Biopsy Assay – on prostate biopsy samples from men who have already been diagnosed and treated for the disease and have at least five years of follow-up data. The study will compare how well the predictions made by the AI tool match what happened to patients in the real world. It will then be tested in real clinics on biopsies from men as they are diagnosed, with a focus on cases in which doctors find it hard to decide the best course of', 'Atlanta, GA Lip Fillers for Fuller, Natural-Looking Lips: Treatments Announced Oculus Plastic Surgery (404-256-1500) announces lip filler injections as a non-surgical option for lip enhancement, reflecting rising demand for minimally invasive aesthetic treatments focused on proportion, symmetry, and reversible facial volume adjustment. Atlanta, United States, April 24, 2026 -- Oculus Plastic Surgery recently announced the availability of lip filler injections as part of its non-surgical facial aesthetic services, offering patients a minimally invasive option to enhance lip volume, definition, and overall facial balance. The procedure involves carefully placed injections of derm', "Mammograms may reveal hidden heart risks for women, Penn State study finds Routine mammograms are best known as a front-line tool for detecting breast cancer. But new research suggests the same X-ray images may also offer an early warning sign for cardiovascular disease — the leading cause of death among women. A study presented at a Dec. 3 Radiological Society of North America meeting found that calcium deposits visible in the arteries of the breast can help predict a woman’s future risk of heart attack, heart failure, stroke and death. Name: 18_santa_christmas_holiday season_festive
Representative_Docs: ['Celebrating Christmas in and around Larnaca The city, the suburbs and the villages of Larnaca all get into the festive mood this season as dozens of Christmas events pop up. All around the Larnaca region, markets, workshops and performances will share magical moments for visitors. The SanTa Lefkara Christmas Village is on until January 6 with artists’ stalls, entertainment, craft workshops, children’s activities and concerts taking over the village centre. As one of the deputy ministry of tourism’s eight Christmas Villages, Lefkara is dressed in full holiday glam with glimmering string lights, Christmas trees and a rich, weekly entertainment programme. On December’s first weekend coming up, Larnaca region’s biggest fair returns. The Traditional Christmas Market makes its anticipated annual comeback at Forest Beach, Dekeleia Road, bringing over 200 stalls selling a wide range of food, craft and handmade items from handbags to sweets, candles, baked goods and hundreds of other unique items. The market will include Santa visits, live radio links, hot drinks and food stands. The Livadia Christmas Programme will also bring holiday cheer on December 6, 14, 20 and 28 as the community square fills with music and dance beats, the fam', 'Want an ‘ezbacke uvin’ or ‘mot cantrl’ for Christmas? Write to Santa The time has come again for both the young and old to send in their letters to Santa. This has become one of my favorite Christmas traditions. The letters to Santa we publish each December are often heartwarming and occasionally heartbreaking. They are a reminder of what makes Christmas magical. They are an amusing break from the sometimes jarring news we must print in the paper. And, hey, they might even be beneficial to your brain’s elasticity. It takes some creativity on the reader’s part to figure out just what the little kiddies are asking for, since we publish the letters verbatim. Try to decipher these phrases from previous Santa letters: I’m looking forward to the, umm, creative writing in this year’s batch of letters. We will publish Santa letters from children and adults in north central Idaho and southeastern Washington. The letters can be just a few words if you have a short wish list, but please try to write no more than 300 words. Be sure to include the author’s name, age and town of residence. The letters can be emailed to city@lmtribune.com, sent in the mail to Letters to Santa, Lewiston Tribune, 505 Capital St., Lewiston, ID 83501, or dropped off at our office. Name: 19_garden_flower_flowers_gardeners
Representative_Docs: ['Attract bees and butterflies to garden with ‘superplant’ bursting with nectar-rich flowers Gardeners can attract bees and butterflies into their outdoor spaces with a nectar-rich "superplant" beloved by wildlife. As spring continues to unfold, green-fingered readers will be heading outdoors to tend to their gardens—made all the more rewarding when wildlife appears to enjoy the results. Pollinators like bees and butterflies play a crucial role in our ecosystem, supporting the growth of plants, fruit and vegetables. They\'re also synonymous with summer and a joy to watch as they flit from flower to flower. The Royal Horticultural Society (RHS) has named a "striking and versatile" wildflower that can help tempt these valuable insects into your own garden, calling it a "top plant we can all grow to support biodiversity". Montebello Park was abuzz as the St. Catharines Horticultural Society and the City unveiled an interpretive panel to honour William Bartlett Burgoyne for his efforts in funding Canada’s first civic rose garden. Burgoyne, a past President of the St. Catharines Horticultural Society, now the Garden City Gardeners, and founder of the St. Catharines Standard started the rose garden with a donation of $1,000 in 1919, an equivalent of over $19,000 in 2026. Tucked in the back of Montebello Park, near the intersection of Lake and Queen streets, the rose garden held a long-kept secret, until a St. Catharines Horticultural Society member revealed it. What started as an interest quickly became a desire to honour the past president, as Jan Harte, recent past president, investigated the garden’s history. “I’ve been very interested in the rose garden, it’s a beautiful centre piece for the park and for the city,” she said. “Then when I found out it wa", "Montebello Park's rose garden gets plaque honouring William Burgoyne's efforts Mayor Mat Siscoe and Jan Harte, St. Catharines Horticultural Society, now the Garden City Gardeners past president, unveil the plaque honouring William Bartless Burgoyne for his efforts in starting Canada’s first civic rose garden in St. Catharines. Name: 20_sam_rebounds_bulldogs_team led
Representative_Docs: ["Samford defeats Reinhardt 85-49 BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — Judson Bjornstad's 16 points helped Samford defeat NAIA member Reinhardt 85-49 on Friday. Bjornstad also contributed six rebounds for the Bulldogs (7-6). Dylan Faulkner scored 15 points and added seven rebounds. Zach Lamey went 5 of 10 from the field (3 for 8 from 3-point range) to finish with 14 points. Tee Williams led the way for the Eagles with 14 points, eight rebounds and two steals. AJ Shaw added eight points, six rebounds and four assists for Reinhardt, and Blake Seitz finished with six points and two steals.
